generatedNote: Honest capability evidence registry. Statuses reflect VERIFIED reality, not aspiration. Under ADR 0056 the primary substrate is NATIVE WINDOWS (WSL2 reframed optional/future); the manual provider-login hard stop is already satisfied — Codex 0.101.0 and Claude 2.1.195 are installed AND authenticated natively on Windows (verified 2026-06-30 via official status commands; no tokens read; PII redacted). Mandatory windows_* capabilities must reach verified_real_environment (real-host OS behavior) or verified_real_provider (real CLI runs) before the final operational 1.0; a CI fixture is not sufficient. Source probe: `pnpm triforge:doctor` on Windows 11 (build 26200), NTFS, 2026-06-30. Updated as each A10-W PR lands.

evidence: pnpm triforge:doctor on Windows 11 (10.0.26200, x64): 18 checks, 0 blockers, 1 long-path warning
Node v24.12.0 / pnpm 11.5.0 / corepack 0.34.5 / Git 2.52.0.windows.1; PostgreSQL 18 service Running, localhost:5432 reachable; repo on C: NTFS
risks: LongPathsEnabled=0 (admin to flip); mitigate git ops with core.longpaths=true
capabilities.notes: A10-W.1: the native Windows toolchain (Node/pnpm/corepack/Git/PostgreSQL) is present and both providers are authenticated, verified by `pnpm triforge:doctor` on a real Windows 11 host. Scope: substrate/toolchain readiness only — the full product run is windows_integrated_product_e2e.

evidence: apps/api/src/execution/path/pathPolicy.ts (the WIRED write gate: Windows lexical pre-gate + case-insensitive containment)
apps/api/src/test/windowsPathPolicy.host.test.ts (standalone validateContainedPath on real NTFS: mklink /J junction escape, ADS, reserved names, traversal, .git) — green on Windows 11 26200

apps/api/src/test/pathPolicy.windows.host.test.ts (the WIRED PathPolicyEngine.checkWrite/checkRead on real NTFS: junction escape, ADS, reserved names, UNC, traversal, .git, case-insensitive containment) — green on Windows 11 26200
risks: post-validation tail-junction TOCTOU: a junction planted at a not-yet-existing tail segment AFTER the check is not caught (RR-2 residual; full TOCTOU hardening is future). Reparse points present AT check time are denied.

AppExecLink / cloud-placeholder reparse tags are not tag-inspected (symlink/junction/mount-point are covered via lstat); admin-only symbolic links are not exercised in the matrix (directory junctions are).
case folding is ASCII-correct (toLowerCase), an approximation of the NTFS $UpCase table — the containment guarantee assumes a system-controlled (ASCII) worktree root.
the forbiddenRoots layer is defense-in-depth only; under containment it is largely inert (a target outside the worktree is already denied).
capabilities.notes: A10-W.2: deny-by-default Windows containment — volume identity + canonical (realpath) resolution of the nearest existing ancestor + case-folded segment-boundary containment (never a raw startsWith). Blocks UNC/extended-length/device namespaces, ADS, reserved device names, trailing dot/space, and junction/reparse escapes present at check time (incl. a nonexistent child below a hostile junction), plus .git. An inaccessible existing segment fails safe (denied_reparse_point). Verified on a REAL Windows host (NTFS, real mklink /J junctions) for BOTH the standalone WindowsExecutionPlatform.validateContainedPath AND the WIRED write gate PathPolicyEngine (which actually authorizes provider writes via roleEnforcer/writableRun); POSIX behavior unchanged. CI (Linux) runs the cross-platform lexical matrix; the real-NTFS reparse behavior is the local Windows-host run. Scope: lexical + existing-chain canonical containment — not yet end-to-end through a real provider write (that is the A10-W.7 pilots).

risks: MAX_PATH truncation; junction protection; locks/stale/crash recovery on NTFS
capabilities.notes: A10-W.3: NTFS worktree manager rooted at %LOCALAPPDATA%\TriForge; one branch per run, never on main; tested against real Git for Windows.

risks: process breakaway; orphan; PID reuse; Job Object lifecycle
capabilities.notes: A10-W.4: native process-tree supervision via Windows Job Objects (kill-on-job-close), replacing POSIX process groups. ExecutionPlatform.createManagedProcess/terminateProcessTree declared (throws until A10-W.4). taskkill /T /F is a documented fallback only.

risks: ACL/restricted-token are not a perfect sandbox (honest residual risk); no setrlimit on Windows
capabilities.notes: A10-W.5: layered Windows defense (worktree + path policy + ACL + restricted token + Job Object + env allowlist + safe command policy + audit). Extends the 13-invariant matrix (ADR 0055) to Windows; residual risk registered.

capabilities.notes: A10-W.8 Competitive mode with real providers on Windows. Per DoD it may be formally excluded from 1.0 by ADR if blocked by real quota; therefore not mandatoryForFinal. Must never be reported verified_real_provider unless actually run.

capabilities.notes: A10.3 capability-gated writable profile. Refuses readOnly:false unless an observed write=yes snapshot (matching version + provider), a 6-field A0.5 binding, and a worktree cwd all hold; authorized run builds `--sandbox workspace-write` and passes the conformance harness. Read-only default unchanged.

capabilities.notes: A10.3 capability-gated writable profile. Authorized run builds `--permission-mode acceptEdits` (never --bare); refuses honestly when write is not observed. Read-only default unchanged.

risks: fixture-driven; the real provider streams are the real-provider closure (codex_windows_*/claude_windows_*)
capabilities.notes: A10.4 writable conformance: both real adapters driven (with fixture writable profiles) through the conformance harness across the failure surface (malformed/unknown/rate-limit/timeout/cancellation/crash) — single terminal, errors normalized, no events after terminal, no secret leakage; plus conformant refusal streams for capability invalidation and worktree-cwd denial.

risks: shared .git/objects store across worktrees (RR; path policy blocks .git)
capabilities.notes: Isolated git linked-worktrees outside the base repo; protected-branch refusal; symlink-escape and git-hook neutralization verified against real fixtures. The Windows/NTFS real-host proof is windows_worktree_manager (A10-W.3).

risks: no OS-level syscall/network namespace confinement in 1.0 (RR-4 residual; future hardening)
capabilities.notes: A10.2 real isolation boundary verified by a 13-invariant fixture matrix (in-process policy composition is the mechanism; ADR 0055). The Windows real-host extension is windows_isolation_boundary (A10-W.5).

Allowlist containment: traversal, symlink-escape, hardlink-clobber, absolute-path, cross-worktree, .git all denied; maxFiles budget enforced. The Windows-specific policy is windows_path_policy (A10-W.2).

Deny-by-default command classification; shell disabled (metacharacters inert); destructive/privileged/network denied by default; denied command never spawns. Windows shells (powershell/pwsh/cmd) + .cmd/PATHEXT/DLL-search hardening are part of windows_isolation_boundary (A10-W.5).
Timeout, output byte cap, and process-group cancellation (SIGTERM->SIGKILL on POSIX; taskkill /T on Windows today). The native Job Object replacement is windows_job_object_supervision (A10-W.4).
Hash-chained, tamper-evident, secret-redacted file-mutation ledger; reconciliation detects out-of-band changes. OS-independent.
Structured gate results from real exit codes, bound to the tested diff hash. OS-independent.
Bounded repair loop (rounds/wall-time/commands/files/quota) with terminal states; mock-driven verification. OS-independent.
Re-derived verdict; decision binding to diff/ledger/gate hashes prevents replay and post-decision modification. OS-independent.
Capacity never fabricated (unknown stays null); reserves protect critical phases; hard stop works without a balance signal; isBillingAuthoritative always false. OS-independent.

risks: view-model level only; not a full browser E2E
capabilities.notes: 8-panel product UI view-models; honest states, no invented backend state, untrusted content sanitized. The real integrated browser+backend run is windows_integrated_product_e2e (A10-W.8).
Run reconstruction and recovery after a process restart (CI fixture). The real-host proof is windows_restart_recovery (A10-W.8).

A10.11 + A10-W.1: the release gate evaluates this machine-readable registry; final-operational readiness requires verified_real_provider for real-provider capabilities and verified_real_environment for real-host capabilities (requiresRealEnvironment). No false green.
